results.question: Does README.md correctly list Codex in the Frameworks & Tools table with accurate description and requirements?

evidence.findings: Codex entry exists at line 82 with exact description 'AI-assisted development CLI (OpenAI)' and Node.js requirement

results.examiner_notes: Table formatting valid and consistent with other framework entries

results.question: Does CHANGELOG.md contain an entry for Codex under [Unreleased] / Added section?

evidence.findings: Comprehensive Codex entry under [Unreleased]/Added describing npm installation, default framework status

results.examiner_notes: Entry includes all required elements: installation method, scope, relationship to existing frameworks

results.question: Does specs/spec.md consistently reference codex in all relevant framework lists?

evidence.findings: Codex appears in all 5 framework list occurrences: wizard prompts, config schema, role selection logic, auto-bundled roles, Ansible roles table

results.examiner_notes: 100% coverage of framework lists, node auto-dependency properly documented

results.question: Is the documentation consistent with the implementation in treating codex as a non-configurable default?

evidence.findings: After fix: specs/spec.md now correctly marks codex as 'always included (non-configurable default)' matching implementation. CHANGELOG correctly states 'default framework' and 'automatically included'.

results.examiner_notes: Minor documentation inconsistency fixed - comment updated from 'optional' to 'always included'

results.question: Are all user-facing documents updated to reflect the codex addition?

evidence.findings: All three user-facing documents comprehensively updated with accurate codex information

results.examiner_notes: Complete documentation coverage, mutually consistent references, no gaps identified
